generic-codec.portable_semantics: Rust generic key and value codecs become typed host wrapper constructors or synchronous codec callbacks with stable decode errors.
generic-codec.performance_contract: Callback results are copied into Rust-owned buffers before return; retained sessions and packed pages bypass per-record codec callbacks on hot reads.

language_patterns.python: codec protocol objects passed to typed map factories
language_patterns.go: generic codec interfaces passed to typed map constructors
language_patterns.node: Codec<T> objects passed to typed map factories
language_patterns.kotlin: Codec<T> interfaces passed to typed map factories
language_patterns.java: Codec<T> interfaces passed to typed map factories
language_patterns.ruby: codec objects responding to encode and decode
language_patterns.swift: Codec protocols passed to typed map initializers
language_patterns.wasm: Codec<T> JavaScript objects passed to typed map factories

iterator-sequence.portable_semantics: Rust iterators become ordered host iterables or sequences for cold paths and bounded cursor pages for hot multi-result paths.
iterator-sequence.performance_contract: Hot reads return bounded packed pages decoded through retained sessions; no Rust iterator borrow or foreign pointer outlives its call or page lease.

language_patterns.go: iterator callback or paged cursor with Close
language_patterns.node: Iterable or AsyncIterable backed by disposable page cursors
language_patterns.wasm: Iterable or AsyncIterable over bounded browser-safe pages

borrowed-view.portable_semantics: Rust lifetime-bound byte and record views become callback-scoped views or explicit page leases whose use after scope closure is rejected.
borrowed-view.performance_contract: Views borrow stable Rust-owned memory only within the synchronous callback or lease; scoped views never cross await and owned fallbacks copy once.

language_patterns.python: context-managed memoryview or callback-scoped bytes view
language_patterns.go: callback-scoped byte slice or closeable page lease
language_patterns.node: callback-scoped Buffer view or disposable page lease
language_patterns.kotlin: callback-scoped ByteBuffer or closeable page lease
language_patterns.java: callback-scoped ByteBuffer or AutoCloseable page lease
language_patterns.ruby: block-scoped string view or explicit page lease
language_patterns.swift: withUnsafeBytes-style closure or scoped page lease
language_patterns.wasm: callback-scoped Uint8Array view invalidated on scope exit

store-trait.portable_semantics: Rust storage traits become host callback or protocol interfaces with the same ordered batch, manifest, transaction, hint, and capability behavior.
store-trait.performance_contract: Inputs are owned for the callback duration, callback results become Rust-owned before return, ordered batch operations stay batched, and async adapters own handles across await.

language_patterns.python: store protocol object with synchronous and async adapters
language_patterns.go: Store interface implemented by host callbacks
language_patterns.node: Store callback object with Promise-based async adapter
language_patterns.kotlin: Store interface with suspend-compatible async adapter
language_patterns.java: Store interface with CompletionStage async adapter
language_patterns.ruby: store object responding to the documented callback methods
language_patterns.swift: Store protocol with async wrapper ownership
language_patterns.wasm: browser Store callback object with Promise-based adapter

builder-typestate.portable_semantics: Rust generic and typestate builders become host builders or immutable option records validated before the Rust object is constructed.
builder-typestate.performance_contract: Validation occurs once at construction; immutable validated configuration is retained in Rust and not rebuilt on each operation.

language_patterns.python: keyword option records validated by factory methods
language_patterns.node: typed option objects and validating factories
language_patterns.java: immutable option records and validating builders
language_patterns.ruby: keyword option hashes normalized by validating factories
language_patterns.wasm: typed JavaScript option objects validated at the WASM boundary

marker-and-associated-type.portable_semantics: Rust marker traits and associated types become documented host type constraints plus stable runtime validation at the first behaviorally relevant boundary.
marker-and-associated-type.performance_contract: Compile-time-only markers add no boundary work; runtime validation is performed once during construction or callback registration.

language_patterns.python: documented protocol type constraints with construction-time validation
language_patterns.go: interface type constraints with construction-time validation
language_patterns.node: TypeScript type constraints with runtime boundary validation
language_patterns.kotlin: generic interface constraints with construction-time validation
language_patterns.java: generic interface constraints with construction-time validation
language_patterns.ruby: documented duck-type constraints with registration-time validation
language_patterns.swift: protocol constraints with throwing construction-time validation
language_patterns.wasm: TypeScript type constraints with WASM boundary validation

callback-protocol.portable_semantics: Rust closure aliases and behavioral callback traits become host callable or protocol values with stable input records, result records, and callback error translation.
callback-protocol.performance_contract: Borrowed callback inputs are valid only for the synchronous call; returned data is Rust-owned before the callback returns and callbacks never execute while a registry lock is held.

language_patterns.python: callable or protocol object receiving immutable callback records
language_patterns.go: function type or callback interface receiving owned records
language_patterns.node: typed function or callback object receiving immutable records
language_patterns.kotlin: function type or single-method callback interface
language_patterns.java: functional interface receiving immutable callback records
language_patterns.ruby: Proc or object responding to the documented callback method
language_patterns.swift: escaping closure or callback protocol receiving value records
language_patterns.wasm: JavaScript function receiving copied or callback-scoped records

record-alias.portable_semantics: Rust tuple and concrete type aliases become named host records or the existing aliased host record without exposing Rust tuple indexing.
record-alias.performance_contract: Owned fields copy once; aliases of packed-page records retain the same bounded page and lease behavior as their canonical record.

language_patterns.go: named struct or canonical aliased struct
language_patterns.node: TypeScript interface or canonical record type
language_patterns.kotlin: data class or canonical record type
language_patterns.java: record class or canonical record type
language_patterns.ruby: immutable value object or canonical record type
language_patterns.swift: value struct or canonical record type
language_patterns.wasm: TypeScript interface or canonical browser-safe record type

namespace-module.portable_semantics: A Rust public module is represented by the host package or namespace containing its application-facing exports; the module path itself has no runtime operation.
namespace-module.performance_contract: Namespace mapping adds no runtime work or boundary allocation.
